metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Maria's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Maria swept the floor carefully.

Anna poured the milk into the small saucepan in the quiet kitchen. Anna placed the small saucepan on the gas stove and lit a flame. The milk boiled over the rim of the small saucepan onto the gas stove. Anna wiped the gas stove clean with a damp kitchen cloth.

The sharp nail lay on the narrow road outside the old school. The front tire of the bicycle rolled over the sharp nail. The front tire of the bicycle lost all air within a few seconds. Peter pushed the bicycle slowly home along the narrow road.

Lena pressed a small seed into the dark soil of the flower pot. Lena poured a cup of water onto the dark soil of the flower pot. A green shoot rose from the dark soil of the flower pot. Lena moved the flower pot onto the sunny windowsill in the kitchen.

Dark clouds gathered above the small garden behind the yellow farmhouse. Heavy rain fell onto the laundry in the small garden. The laundry turned completely wet within a few short minutes. Ingrid carried the laundry into the warm kitchen of the yellow farmhouse.

Jonas wrote a long letter to the old friend in Lisbon. Jonas dropped the long letter into the yellow postbox on the corner. A postal van collected the long letter from the yellow postbox. The old friend in Lisbon read the long letter twice.

Marta mixed flour, water, and yeast in the wooden bowl on the counter. Marta kneaded the dough in the wooden bowl for ten long minutes. The dough rose slowly in the wooden bowl beside the hot oven. Marta shaped the dough into a round loaf for the hot oven.

Cold frost covered the windows of the old car in the driveway. The engine of the old car refused to start for Daniel. Daniel connected jumper cables between the old car and a neighbour's van. The engine of the old car started with a sudden loud roar.

The grey cat jumped onto the narrow shelf above the old fireplace. The grey cat pushed the blue vase to the edge of the narrow shelf. The blue vase fell onto the stone floor and shattered into pieces. Klara swept the stone floor beneath the narrow shelf with a broom.

The strong storm shook the tall oak beside the wooden fence all night. The heavy branch broke away from the tall oak and fell down. The heavy branch crushed a section of the wooden fence. Bernd cut the heavy branch into short pieces with a saw.

Sofia carried the new phone in the pocket of a light jacket. The new phone slipped from the pocket into the cold garden pond. Sofia pulled the new phone out of the cold garden pond quickly. The screen of the new phone stayed completely dark for the whole evening.

The shoelace of the left boot came loose on the narrow forest path. Ben stepped on the shoelace and stumbled hard toward the ground. Ben landed on the wet mud of the narrow forest path. Ben tied the shoelace with a firm double knot and stood up.

The tall candle burned on the wooden table in the dark room. The wax ran down the side of the tall candle. The wax dripped onto the wooden table and hardened into a small lump. Hanna scraped the wax off the wooden table with a blunt knife.

Emil bought the chocolate ice cream at the small kiosk in the park. The chocolate ice cream melted quickly under the strong summer sun. The chocolate ice cream dripped onto the white shirt of Emil. Emil washed the white shirt in the bathroom sink at home.

The red balloon floated above the wooden bench in the busy park. The red balloon touched a sharp thorn on the rose bush. The red balloon burst with a loud bang above the wooden bench. A small child cried beside the wooden bench for several minutes.

Paul poured warm water over the yeast in the mixing bowl. Paul stirred flour into the mixing bowl with a wooden spoon. The dough in the mixing bowl doubled in size within an hour. Paul punched the dough down with a floured fist and waited.

Ruth held a long nail against the white wall of the hallway. Ruth struck the long nail with a heavy hammer several times. The long nail sank deep into the white wall of the hallway. Ruth hung a framed photograph of a mountain on the long nail.

The paint tin stood open on the top step of the ladder. Marco's elbow knocked the paint tin off the top step. The paint tin fell onto the grey carpet in the living room. White paint spread across the grey carpet in a wide circle.

A sudden gust of wind lifted the straw hat from Nora's head. The straw hat flew across the wide beach toward the water. The straw hat landed in the shallow pool near the water. Nora waded into the shallow pool and grabbed the straw hat.

The brown dog dug a deep hole in the vegetable patch. The brown dog pulled a rubber bone out of the deep hole. Loose earth from the deep hole covered the young carrot plants. Oskar filled the deep hole and flattened the vegetable patch with a rake.

Felix kicked the leather ball hard across the narrow street toward the bakery. The leather ball struck the front window of the bakery. The front window of the bakery broke into many small pieces. The leather ball landed among the bread rolls inside the bakery.

Julia set the coffee cup beside the black keyboard on the desk. Julia's sleeve caught the coffee cup during a long phone call. Hot coffee spilled from the coffee cup across the black keyboard. The black keyboard stopped working completely within a few short seconds.

Heavy rain fell on the mountains above the small river for days. The small river rose above the low banks near the village. Brown water from the small river flooded the market square. Villagers stacked sandbags along the low banks of the small river.

Thick snow covered the steep driveway in front of the closed garage. Greta pushed a wide shovel through the thick snow on the steep driveway. Greta piled the thick snow against the low wall beside the closed garage. The steep driveway lay clear and dark under the grey winter sky.

A sharp pain started in the back tooth of Simon. Simon called the dental clinic on the main street of the town. The dentist examined the back tooth of Simon with a small mirror. The dentist filled the back tooth of Simon with white cement.

A rough plank on the old bench scratched Rosa's palm. The wooden splinter stayed deep under the skin of Rosa's palm. Rosa pulled the wooden splinter out with a pair of small tweezers. Rosa pressed a clean bandage over the small wound on Rosa's palm.

The bee landed on the bare arm of Lukas in the meadow. The bee stung the bare arm of Lukas near the elbow. The bare arm of Lukas swelled around the small red sting. A neighbour pressed a cold cloth against the bare arm of Lukas.

Vera searched the coat pockets for the house keys outside the front door. The house keys lay somewhere inside the locked apartment behind the front door. Vera called the locksmith from the phone of a friendly neighbour. The locksmith opened the front door of the locked apartment within ten minutes.

Two slices of bread stayed too long inside the old toaster. Thick smoke rose from the old toaster into the small kitchen. The smoke alarm above the small kitchen screamed at full volume. Adam opened every window in the small kitchen and unplugged the old toaster.

The kitchen tap dripped steadily into the metal sink for weeks. A small puddle formed under the metal sink on the tile floor. Elsa called the plumber about the kitchen tap and the metal sink. The plumber replaced the worn washer inside the kitchen tap.

Miriam opened the flat cardboard box from the large furniture shop. Miriam laid the wooden parts of the bookshelf on the living room floor. Miriam screwed the wooden parts of the bookshelf together with a small screwdriver. The bookshelf stood upright against the wall of the living room.

Tall weeds grew between the rows of lettuce in the vegetable garden. Karl pulled the tall weeds out of the vegetable garden by hand. Karl carried the tall weeds to the compost heap behind the shed. The rows of lettuce stood clean and open in the vegetable garden.

The ripe apple hung from a high branch of the old tree. The ripe apple fell into the tall grass under the old tree. A wasp crawled into the ripe apple in the tall grass. Hilde picked the ripe apple up and threw the ripe apple away.

The paper kite climbed high above the empty field in strong wind. The string of the paper kite snapped near the wooden handle. The paper kite drifted toward the line of trees at the edge. The paper kite hung in the top branches of a tall poplar.

Nils gathered dry sticks along the shore of the dark lake. Nils built the small fire with the dry sticks near the water. The small fire burned brightly beside the shore of the dark lake. Nils poured cold lake water over the small fire and walked home.

Ida cast the fishing line far into the calm lake from the boat. The large pike took the bait on the fishing line. Ida pulled the large pike into the boat with a net. Ida released the large pike back into the calm lake.

Hugo emptied the laundry basket into the drum of the washing machine. Hugo added detergent and started the washing machine at sixty degrees. The washing machine finished the long cycle with a short beep. Hugo hung the wet clothes on the line in the back garden.

Clara sat down in the tall chair of the small hair salon. The hairdresser cut long strands from the hair of Clara. Loose hair covered the tiled floor around the tall chair. Clara paid the hairdresser at the counter of the small hair salon.

Yusuf pulled the zip of the winter jacket upward with sudden force. The zip of the winter jacket jammed halfway up the front. The metal slider of the zip came away in Yusuf's fingers. Yusuf took the winter jacket to a tailor on the market street.

The red paint can stood on a newspaper beside the white door. Tobias stumbled over the red paint can in the narrow hallway. The red paint flowed from the red paint can across the wooden floor. Tobias soaked the red paint up with a pile of old rags.

Jana lay on a wide towel beside the pool for three hours. The strong sun burned the shoulders of Jana a deep red. The skin on the shoulders of Jana peeled away in thin flakes. A friend rubbed cool lotion onto the shoulders of Jana.

The alarm clock of Viktor stayed silent inside the dark bedroom. Viktor ran to the station without breakfast or a warm coat. The express train left the station in front of Viktor. Viktor waited on the cold platform of the station for another train.

A driver left the heavy parcel at the door of the apartment. Rain soaked the cardboard of the heavy parcel for several hours. The cardboard of the heavy parcel tore open at the bottom. Rita carried the heavy parcel inside the apartment with both arms.

The fern stood in the blue pot in the empty office all summer. The blue pot received no water for many weeks in the empty office. The fern turned brown and dry at the edges of every leaf. A cleaner carried the blue pot and the fern out of the empty office.

Anton walked across the wet field behind the barn in old boots. Thick mud covered the old boots up to the ankle. Anton scraped the thick mud off the old boots with a flat stick. Anton left the old boots outside the door of the barn.

Nadia took the brown egg from the carton on the counter. Nadia tapped the brown egg against the rim of the glass bowl. The shell of the brown egg cracked along a thin line. Nadia emptied the brown egg into the glass bowl and whisked hard.

The old printer pulled three sheets of paper in at once. The paper jammed inside the old printer with a loud grinding noise. Gustav opened the back cover of the old printer with care. Gustav pulled the paper out of the old printer with both hands.

Nina turned the thin page of the old atlas much too quickly. The thin page of the old atlas tore from top to bottom. Nina laid the thin page flat on the wooden table in the study. Nina joined the thin page with a long strip of clear tape.

The pair of swallows carried mud to the beam of the old barn. The pair of swallows built a round nest on the beam. Four eggs lay in the round nest on the beam of the old barn. Four young swallows left the round nest at the end of summer.

Two children rolled the three large snowballs across the white garden. The three large snowballs formed the tall snowman near the garden gate. Warm wind blew across the white garden for two whole days. The tall snowman sank into a low heap beside the garden gate.

Otto leaned the wet umbrella against the wall of the narrow hallway. The puddle spread from the wet umbrella across the wooden floor. Otto stepped into the puddle with a clean woollen sock. Otto hung the wet umbrella on a hook above the wooden floor.

The kettle whistled loudly on the small camping stove inside the tent. Petra lifted the kettle off the small camping stove with a cloth. Petra poured hot water from the kettle into the tin mug. Steam rose from the tin mug into the cold air of the tent.

The heavy suitcase stood on the top shelf of the tall wardrobe. Silke reached for the heavy suitcase from a wobbly wooden chair. The heavy suitcase slid off the top shelf onto the left foot of Silke. Silke limped to the kitchen for a bag of frozen peas.

Rain fell on the flat roof of the wooden cabin all night. Water found the small crack in the flat roof of the wooden cabin. Drops fell from the ceiling onto the bed inside the wooden cabin. Jonas placed a metal bucket on the bed under the small crack.

Emma dropped a coin into the slot of the coffee machine. The coffee machine ground the dark beans with a loud rattle. Black coffee ran from the coffee machine into the paper cup. Emma carried the paper cup to a table by the window.

The candle on the birthday cake burned down to the icing. Leon blew out the candle on the birthday cake with one breath. Grey smoke curled from the candle above the birthday cake. Leon cut the birthday cake into eight uneven slices with a knife.

The wooden gate of the paddock stood open in the strong wind. The brown pony walked through the wooden gate onto the country lane. The farmer caught the brown pony near a hedge on the country lane. The farmer led the brown pony back through the wooden gate.

Ellen pushed a heavy trolley through the narrow aisle of the supermarket. The tower of tins stood at the end of the narrow aisle. The heavy trolley struck the tower of tins near the bottom. Tins rolled across the floor of the supermarket in every direction.

Ravi hung the bird feeder from the low branch of the apple tree. Sunflower seeds filled the bird feeder to the very top. A crowd of sparrows found the bird feeder within two days. The bird feeder hung empty from the low branch of the apple tree.

The little boat drifted away from the wooden jetty on the lake. The rope of the little boat trailed loose in the dark water. The swimmer caught the rope of the little boat near the reeds. The swimmer pulled the little boat back to the wooden jetty.

Frida knocked a bottle of ink across the desk in the study. Blue ink spread over the pages of the open notebook. The pages of the open notebook turned dark and soft with ink. Frida threw the open notebook into the wastebasket beside the desk.

The old radio played quietly in the workshop during a heavy thunderstorm. A lightning strike hit the power line above the workshop. Every light in the workshop went dark within a single second. Bruno lit two candles on the bench in the workshop.

Sanna spilled sugar across the kitchen counter beside the open window. The column of ants found the sugar on the kitchen counter quickly. The column of ants stretched from the open window across the kitchen counter. Sanna wiped the sugar away and closed the open window firmly.

The zip line stretched between the two tall pines above the forest floor. A child slid down the zip line with a loud shout. The rope of the zip line stretched and sagged in the middle. A ranger tightened the zip line between the two tall pines.

Elias planted the young pear tree in the corner of the garden. A strong wind bent the young pear tree toward the fence. Elias tied the young pear tree to a wooden stake with cloth. The young pear tree grew straight through the whole warm summer.

Pia dropped the tea bag into the white mug on the tray. Pia poured boiling water over the tea bag in the white mug. The white mug held a deep brown liquid within three minutes. Pia lifted the tea bag out of the white mug with a spoon.

The rusty bolt held the front wheel of the old cart. Ahmed turned the rusty bolt with a large wrench for several minutes. The rusty bolt came free from the front wheel of the old cart. The front wheel of the old cart rolled into the tall grass.

The large moth flew through the open window into the bright room. The large moth circled the ceiling lamp of the bright room. The large moth landed on the hot glass of the ceiling lamp. Freya carried the large moth outside on a sheet of paper.

The heavy book stood at the edge of the top shelf. A tremor from a passing lorry shook the top shelf slightly. The heavy book fell from the top shelf onto the desk lamp. The desk lamp tipped over and the bulb of the desk lamp broke.

Mikko cut a thick slice of onion on the wooden board. The sharp knife slipped on the wooden board into the finger of Mikko. Blood ran from the finger of Mikko onto the wooden board. A plaster from the drawer covered the finger of Mikko.

Greta left the car window open in the crowded car park. A heavy shower soaked the front seats of the car through the car window. Water gathered in the deep hollows of the front seats. Greta pressed a thick towel into the front seats of the car.

The village baker slid the metal tray into the hot oven. The metal tray stayed inside the hot oven for a full hour. The bread rolls on the metal tray turned black and hard. The village baker threw the bread rolls into a large bin.

The stone wall along the field lost mortar over many winters. A heavy cow leaned against the stone wall near the gate. The middle section of the stone wall collapsed into the muddy field. A farmer rebuilt the middle section of the stone wall with fresh mortar.

Wanda placed the wet coat over the radiator in the small room. The radiator under the wet coat grew very hot within minutes. Steam rose from the wet coat toward the ceiling of the small room. The wet coat felt dry and warm to the touch.

The wooden spoon rested against the hot pan on the stove. The handle of the wooden spoon grew black at the lower end. Smoke curled from the handle of the wooden spoon into the room. Timo dropped the wooden spoon into the sink of cold water.

Aisha turned the hot tap of the bath tub in the small bathroom. Aisha walked to the kitchen for a tall glass of juice. The bath tub overflowed onto the tiled floor of the small bathroom. A wide puddle reached the door of the small bathroom.

The old clock on the wall of the hall stopped completely. Mateo opened the back of the old clock with a screwdriver. A dead battery lay inside the back of the old clock. Mateo pushed a fresh battery into the old clock and closed the back.

Erik parked the delivery van under the tall lime tree on the street. Sticky sap dripped from the tall lime tree onto the delivery van. A grey layer of dust stuck to the sticky sap on the delivery van. Erik washed the delivery van with a brush and warm soapy water.

The wind slammed the front door of the cottage with great force. A pane of glass in the front door cracked from corner to corner. Cold air poured through the front door into the narrow hall. Signe taped a sheet of cardboard over the front door.

Lars poured the concrete into the wooden frame beside the garden path. The concrete filled the wooden frame to the upper edge. A cat walked across the concrete inside the wooden frame. Small paw prints stayed in the concrete beside the garden path.

The pipe under the kitchen sink froze during a very cold night. The pipe under the kitchen sink burst near the metal joint. Water sprayed from the pipe into the cupboard under the kitchen sink. Ola closed the main valve in the cellar of the house.

Nora hung the wet painting on a hook in the narrow corridor. The wet painting touched the white wall of the narrow corridor. A long green smear appeared on the white wall of the narrow corridor. Nora painted the white wall again with two coats of paint.

The oak barrel stood full of cider in the cold cellar for months. Wild yeast worked slowly inside the oak barrel through the winter. Pressure built inside the oak barrel and pushed the wooden bung out. Cider sprayed across the stone floor of the cold cellar.

The magnet fell from the fridge door onto the kitchen tiles. The magnet broke into two pieces on the kitchen tiles. A photograph slid from the fridge door to the kitchen tiles. Bea glued the two pieces of the magnet back together.

The lawn behind the house grew tall through a wet spring. Rolf pushed the old mower across the lawn behind the house. The blade of the old mower struck a hidden stone with force. Rolf sharpened the blade of the old mower on a grinding wheel.

The chess clock ticked beside the board in the quiet club room. Anja moved a bishop across the board to a dark square. Anja pressed the chess clock with a firm and steady finger. The opponent of Anja studied the board for a long while.

Water dripped from a pipe onto the cellar steps for weeks. The green moss grew across the cellar steps in the damp air. A visitor slipped on the green moss of the cellar steps. Pekka scrubbed the cellar steps with a stiff brush and bleach.

The salt in the shaker turned hard in the humid kitchen. Ivan shook the shaker over the soup without any result. Ivan opened the shaker and broke the salt with a small fork. The salt fell from the shaker into the hot soup.

The battery of the torch died deep inside the long cave. Tuula stood still in the complete darkness of the long cave. Tuula found a spare battery in the front pocket of the rucksack. The torch lit the rough wall of the long cave once more.

Hares ate the young bark of the apple tree in winter. A wide ring of bark disappeared from the trunk of the apple tree. The branches of the apple tree carried no leaves in spring. A gardener cut the apple tree down to a low stump.

The kite string cut a thin line into the palm of Ines. Ines let the kite string run through the folded cloth. The folded cloth protected the palm of Ines from the kite string. The paper kite climbed higher above the sand of the wide beach.

Grease built up inside the metal pipe below the kitchen sink. The kitchen sink held the dirty water for a very long time. Miguel poured a strong drain cleaner into the kitchen sink. The dirty water disappeared from the kitchen sink within a minute.

The soldering iron heated on the metal stand of the workbench. Kasper touched the tip of the soldering iron to the copper wire. Solder melted onto the copper wire in a small silver bead. Kasper set the soldering iron back on the metal stand.

Mist covered the windscreen of the small car in the car park. Ronja switched the heater of the small car to full power. Warm air blew across the windscreen of the small car. The windscreen of the small car cleared from the middle outward.

The gull grabbed a chip from the paper bag of Wilma. The gull flew to the railing above the harbour with a full beak. The paper bag of Wilma tipped over onto the wooden bench. Wilma gathered the fallen food from the wooden bench into the paper bag.

The rope of the swing hung from a thick branch of the beech. Years of rain rotted the rope of the swing at the top. The rope of the swing snapped under the weight of a child. A parent hung a new rope from the thick branch of the beech.

Sten filled the bird bath with clean water in the front garden. A hard frost covered the front garden through a long cold night. The bird bath held a thick disc of hard grey ice. The bird bath cracked along the rim in the front garden.

A truck carried a tall load under the low railway bridge. The tall load struck the underside of the low railway bridge. Bricks from the tall load fell across the road beneath the low railway bridge. Police closed the road beneath the low railway bridge for two hours.

Salt water splashed over the metal railing of the fishing boat. Rust spread across the metal railing of the fishing boat over months. The metal railing of the fishing boat gave way under a heavy hand. A welder fitted a new railing onto the fishing boat.

Anders wrote the phone number on the back of a paper receipt. The paper receipt went through the wash in a trouser pocket. The ink on the paper receipt faded into a grey smudge. Anders searched the phone book for the phone number for an hour.
noncausal_texts: A student borrowed a heavy atlas from the town library. The town library hosted a small chess tournament in the main hall. A tourist photographed the marble staircase of the town library. A volunteer repaired a wooden shelf at the town library.

A baker dropped a tray of rolls on the floor of the village bakery. The village bakery ordered a new oven from a company in Turin. A cat slept on the windowsill of the village bakery for an hour. A painter repainted the wooden sign above the village bakery.

A keeper polished the great glass lens of the old lighthouse. A storm broke a window in the tower of the old lighthouse. A photographer from a travel magazine visited the old lighthouse for a week. A council inspector measured the stone foundations of the old lighthouse.

A class of children sketched the dinosaur skeleton at the natural history museum. A donor gave a rare fossil to the natural history museum. The natural history museum closed the east wing for urgent repairs. A guard found a lost umbrella near the entrance of the natural history museum.

A caretaker mowed the field behind the village school. The village school received a piano from a family in the town. A pupil broke a window in the gymnasium of the village school. A journalist wrote a short article about the village school.

A cyclist crossed the iron bridge in heavy rain and thick fog. The iron bridge carried a herd of cattle to the north bank. An engineer from the city measured a crack in the iron bridge. A painter covered the railings of the iron bridge with green paint.

A brass band played waltzes in the bandstand of the city park. A gardener planted two hundred tulip bulbs in the city park. A jogger lost a house key on the gravel path of the city park. The city park hosted a kite festival on the wide lawn.

A pigeon nested in the roof of the central station. The central station replaced the departure boards on the main concourse. A busker played the accordion beside the ticket hall of the central station. A conductor found a violin in a luggage rack at the central station.

A hen laid an egg in the hay of the hillside farm. The hillside farm sold a tractor to a neighbour in the valley. A vet from the town treated a lame sheep at the hillside farm. A film crew shot a milk advertisement at the hillside farm.

A guest left a wallet in room twelve of the seaside hotel. The seaside hotel replaced the carpet in the long upstairs corridor. A wedding party filled the dining room of the seaside hotel. A seagull broke a skylight above the kitchen of the seaside hotel.

A sailor ordered black coffee and a warm roll at the harbour café. The harbour café hired a cook from a village in Galicia. A tourist forgot a camera on a table of the harbour café. A storm cracked the front window of the harbour café.

A projectionist repaired a broken reel at the old cinema. The old cinema showed a silent film with live piano music. A schoolboy dropped a bag of popcorn in the old cinema. A council grant paid for new seats at the old cinema.

A researcher measured a giant water lily at the botanical garden. The botanical garden sold rare seeds to a nursery in Kent. A wedding photographer worked in the palm house of the botanical garden. A frost damaged the young magnolia trees at the botanical garden.

A driver lost a set of car keys at the ferry terminal. The ferry terminal installed a new ticket machine near the entrance. A choir from Bergen sang sea songs inside the ferry terminal. A gull nested on the flat roof of the ferry terminal.

A hiker signed the visitor book at the mountain hut. The mountain hut ran out of firewood during a long cold spell. A helicopter delivered fresh supplies to the mountain hut. A marmot dug a burrow beneath the terrace of the mountain hut.

A keeper weighed a young tapir at the city zoo. The city zoo opened a new enclosure for the snow leopards. A visitor lost a woollen scarf near the aviary of the city zoo. A film crew recorded birdsong at the city zoo.

A swimmer broke a district record at the public swimming pool. The public swimming pool replaced the tiles around the shallow end. A child found a lost earring on the floor of the public swimming pool. A heatwave doubled the visitor numbers at the public swimming pool.

A collector bought a brass compass at the flea market. The flea market moved to a car park behind the church. A juggler entertained a small crowd at the flea market. A dealer sold a wooden rocking horse at the flea market.

A carpenter replaced a rotten beam in the wooden church. The wooden church received a new bell from a foundry in Innsbruck. A choir from Riga sang evening songs in the wooden church. A woodpecker damaged the north wall of the wooden church.

A pianist played jazz beside gate nine of the airport terminal. The airport terminal closed a runway for resurfacing work. A passenger left a violin case at the airport terminal. A bird strike delayed a morning flight at the airport terminal.

An apprentice trued a buckled wheel at the bicycle workshop. The bicycle workshop ordered a crate of inner tubes from Taiwan. A thief broke the back door of the bicycle workshop. A retired postman donated an old delivery bike to the bicycle workshop.

A doctoral student grew a crystal in the university laboratory. The university laboratory bought an electron microscope from a firm in Jena. A power cut ruined a batch of samples in the university laboratory. A school group toured the university laboratory on an open day.

A trawler landed a record catch at the fishing harbour. The fishing harbour dredged the silt from the main channel. A painter from Bergen sketched the moored boats at the fishing harbour. A seal swam between the pontoons of the fishing harbour.

A miller ground barley for a local farmer at the old windmill. The old windmill lost a sail in a winter gale. A school class visited the old windmill on a rainy morning. A pair of barn owls nested in the cap of the old windmill.

A picker filled forty crates with grapes at the terraced vineyard. The terraced vineyard bought a new press from a workshop in Bolzano. Hail damaged the young vines at the terraced vineyard. A journalist tasted the new wine at the terraced vineyard.

A family pitched a large tent at the riverside campsite. The riverside campsite installed solar panels above the shower block. A fox stole a sausage from a grill at the riverside campsite. A ranger measured the water level near the riverside campsite.

An intern misspelled a headline at the local newspaper office. The local newspaper office sold the old printing press to a museum. A reader delivered a box of historic photographs to the local newspaper office. A burst pipe flooded the archive of the local newspaper office.

A machinist stitched a hundred leather uppers at the shoe factory. The shoe factory hired twelve workers from a nearby town. A fire drill emptied the shoe factory in four minutes. A designer from Milan visited the shoe factory in autumn.

A nurse recorded the blood pressure of every patient on the hospital ward. The hospital ward received a donation of forty books. A therapy dog visited the hospital ward for an afternoon. A window cleaner washed the tall windows of the hospital ward.

An astronomer photographed a distant comet at the mountain observatory. The mountain observatory replaced the drive motor of the main telescope. A snowstorm blocked the access road to the mountain observatory. A radio journalist interviewed the director of the mountain observatory.

A diver cleaned the inside of the shark tank at the city aquarium. The city aquarium hatched forty seahorses in a breeding room. A toddler pressed a jam sandwich against the glass at the city aquarium. A marine biologist from Lisbon lectured at the city aquarium.

A soprano forgot a line during the second act at the opera house. The opera house replaced the red curtain of the main stage. A tourist photographed the gold ceiling of the opera house. A pigeon flew into the foyer of the opera house.

A mechanic changed the brakes of a city bus at the bus depot. The bus depot painted new numbers on the parking bays. A driver found a lost rucksack at the bus depot. A film company rented the bus depot for a night shoot.

A gardener raised two hundred tomato seedlings in the old greenhouse. The old greenhouse lost three panes in a hailstorm. A biology class counted the pollinating bees in the old greenhouse. A cat raised four kittens under a bench in the old greenhouse.

A skater landed a triple jump at the town ice rink. The town ice rink hired a new coach from Ottawa. A compressor failure softened the ice at the town ice rink. A pensioners' club booked the town ice rink for a morning session.

A cyclist reported a stolen bicycle at the police station. The police station replaced the lights above the front entrance. A schoolgirl handed a lost wallet to the police station. A retired officer gave a talk about road safety at the police station.

A registrar married a young couple at the town hall. The town hall repaired the clock face above the main door. A choir from Ghent performed carols inside the town hall. A photographer documented the painted ceiling of the town hall.

A typesetter dropped a tray of metal letters at the printing house. The printing house bought a folding machine from a firm in Leipzig. An author signed five hundred copies at the printing house. A power failure stopped the presses at the printing house.

A beginner threw a lopsided bowl at the pottery studio. The pottery studio fired a large kiln load of tiles. A supplier delivered a ton of clay to the pottery studio. A cat left paw prints across the wet floor of the pottery studio.

A worker sharpened the main blade at the old sawmill. The old sawmill cut oak beams for a barn in the valley. A journalist filmed a documentary at the old sawmill. A storm brought down the power line to the old sawmill.

A trainee climbed the practice tower at the fire station. The fire station bought a new ladder truck from a maker in Ulm. A class of six year olds toured the fire station. A wasp nest appeared under the roof of the fire station.

A farrier shod four horses at the riding stable. The riding stable ordered twenty bales of hay from a farm in Kildare. A beginner fell from a grey mare at the riding stable. A vet vaccinated the ponies at the riding stable.

A retired teacher planted a row of beans at the community garden. The community garden built a wooden compost bin near the gate. A thief took a watering can from the community garden. A local bakery donated bread for a summer party at the community garden.

A collector found a rare jazz pressing at the record shop. The record shop hosted a live set by a band from Leeds. A pipe leaked above the stockroom of the record shop. A courier delivered a crate of vinyl to the record shop.

A dealer valued a silver teapot at the antique shop. The antique shop moved a heavy oak wardrobe into the front window. A tourist bought a brass telescope at the antique shop. A mouse chewed the corner of a rug in the antique shop.

A ranger counted the breeding cranes at the wildlife reserve. The wildlife reserve fenced a stretch of marsh near the river. A student recorded the frog calls at the wildlife reserve. A wildfire burned a hectare of heath at the wildlife reserve.

A boater dropped a mooring rope into the water at the canal lock. The canal lock closed for repairs to the lower gates. A heron fished from the wall of the canal lock. An artist painted the wooden beams of the canal lock.

A nurse bandaged the paw of a terrier at the veterinary clinic. The veterinary clinic bought an ultrasound scanner from a company in Osaka. A farmer brought an injured calf to the veterinary clinic. A parrot escaped from a carrier inside the veterinary clinic.

A brewer tested the sugar level of the wort at the town brewery. The town brewery installed a new bottling line in the main hall. A tour group tasted four beers at the town brewery. A delivery lorry blocked the yard of the town brewery.

A tailor cut a wool suit for a violinist at the corner tailor shop. The corner tailor shop bought a heavy steam iron from a supplier in Milan. A customer collected a mended coat from the corner tailor shop. A moth damaged a roll of grey cloth at the corner tailor shop.

A skipper repainted the hull of a small yacht at the marina. The marina dredged the entrance channel before the sailing season. A child caught a crab from the pontoon of the marina. A storm tore a sail on a moored boat at the marina.

A cook prepared a pot of goulash at the mountain ski lodge. The mountain ski lodge replaced the boiler in the cellar. A guest lost a glove near the entrance of the mountain ski lodge. An avalanche closed the road below the mountain ski lodge.

A child chose a wooden train at the corner toy shop. The corner toy shop ordered a hundred kites from a workshop in Kraków. A clown handed out balloons outside the corner toy shop. A leaking radiator damaged a box of puzzles at the corner toy shop.

A puppeteer repaired the strings of a wooden knight at the puppet theatre. The puppet theatre performed an old folk tale for a school group. A grandmother donated a box of costumes to the puppet theatre. A stage light burned out during a matinee at the puppet theatre.

An electrician rewired an old tram at the tram depot. The tram depot repainted the yellow lines across the main floor. A photographer documented the historic vehicles at the tram depot. A society of enthusiasts restored a wooden tram at the tram depot.

A clerk weighed a heavy parcel at the village post office. The village post office replaced the counter in the main room. A customer posted a birthday card at the village post office. A power cut stopped the stamp machine at the village post office.

A barber trimmed the beard of a retired sailor at the barber shop. The barber shop installed a mirror above the second chair. A customer left a newspaper on the bench of the barber shop. A supplier delivered a case of razors to the barber shop.

A florist arranged a wedding bouquet at the flower shop. The flower shop received a crate of tulips from a grower in Haarlem. A customer ordered a wreath at the flower shop. A frost damaged the display outside the flower shop.

A farmer sold three sacks of potatoes on the market square. The market square hosted a puppet show for a crowd of children. A pickpocket took a purse on the market square. A council crew replaced the cobbles on the market square.

A backpacker cooked lentil soup at the youth hostel. The youth hostel bought thirty new mattresses from a supplier in Aarhus. A guitarist played folk songs in the common room of the youth hostel. A pipe burst in the basement of the youth hostel.

An angler landed a large bass from the wooden pier. The wooden pier lost a plank in a winter storm. A magician performed card tricks on the wooden pier. A photographer recorded the sunrise from the wooden pier.

A guide led a group through the lower galleries of the salt mine. The salt mine installed a new lift in the main shaft. A geologist collected crystal samples at the salt mine. A concert of chamber music took place inside the salt mine.

A presenter interviewed a novelist at the local radio station. The local radio station replaced the antenna on the roof. A listener sent a box of homemade biscuits to the local radio station. A thunderstorm knocked the local radio station off the air.

A stonemason repaired the front steps of the county courthouse. The county courthouse installed a lift beside the main staircase. A photographer documented the wooden panelling of the county courthouse. A school group watched a mock trial at the county courthouse.

A farmer pruned the oldest trees in the olive grove. The olive grove produced two hundred litres of oil. A photographer from Athens documented the harvest at the olive grove. A drought cracked the dry soil of the olive grove.

A beekeeper harvested forty jars of honey at the hillside apiary. The hillside apiary lost two colonies over a cold winter. A journalist filmed the swarming bees at the hillside apiary. A carpenter built ten new hive boxes for the hillside apiary.

A ranger repaired a broken radio at the forest ranger station. The forest ranger station received a delivery of fire beaters. A hiker asked for a trail map at the forest ranger station. A bear damaged a rubbish bin behind the forest ranger station.

A historian read a medieval charter at the city archive. The city archive digitised a collection of old maps. A leaking gutter dampened a wall of the city archive. A donor gave a bundle of wartime letters to the city archive.

A lecturer explained the phases of the moon at the planetarium. The planetarium replaced the projector in the main dome. A school group watched a show about comets at the planetarium. A composer wrote music for a new show at the planetarium.

A clockmaker oiled the great mechanism inside the clock tower. The clock tower lost a hand from the north face. A council crew repainted the roof of the clock tower. A tourist climbed the spiral stairs of the clock tower.

A driller cut a block of granite at the stone quarry. The stone quarry supplied paving for a square in Turin. A geology class studied the rock layers at the stone quarry. A heavy rain flooded the lower level of the stone quarry.

An engineer replaced a roller at the riverside paper mill. The riverside paper mill produced a batch of handmade sheets. A school class toured the riverside paper mill. A frost froze the water intake of the riverside paper mill.

A glassblower shaped a tall vase at the village glassworks. The village glassworks relined the main furnace with new bricks. A collector bought a set of blue tumblers at the village glassworks. An apprentice from Murano trained at the village glassworks.

A welder joined two hull plates at the old shipyard. The old shipyard launched a wooden ketch into the river. A photographer recorded the dry dock at the old shipyard. A gale bent a crane arm at the old shipyard.

A volunteer polished a brass nameplate at the train museum. The train museum acquired a steam locomotive from a depot in Wolsztyn. A model club exhibited a large layout at the train museum. A leak damaged the ceiling above the ticket desk of the train museum.

An engineer inspected the spillway of the upland reservoir. A drought exposed the ruins of a village at the upland reservoir. A rowing club held a regatta on the upland reservoir. An osprey caught a trout at the upland reservoir.

A painter repainted the tank of the old water tower. The old water tower housed a mobile phone mast on the roof. A photographer climbed the ladder of the old water tower. A falcon hunted pigeons from the old water tower.

A mechanic repaired the carousel motor at the fairground. The fairground hosted a competition for the largest pumpkin. A child lost a stuffed rabbit at the fairground. A storm blew down a canvas stall at the fairground.

An acrobat practised a somersault under the circus tent. The circus tent lost a guy rope in a strong wind. A brass quartet rehearsed inside the circus tent. A veterinarian examined the horses beside the circus tent.

A counsellor taught knot tying at the lakeside summer camp. The lakeside summer camp built a wooden raft for the swimming area. A child found an arrowhead near the lakeside summer camp. A thunderstorm cancelled the canoe trip at the lakeside summer camp.

A monk copied a page of a psalter at the hillside monastery. The hillside monastery repaired the roof above the refectory. A choir from Ferrara sang vespers at the hillside monastery. A scholar catalogued the old books at the hillside monastery.

A gardener divided a clump of lemon balm at the herb garden. The herb garden supplied mint to a restaurant in the old town. A photographer documented the flowering sage at the herb garden. A hailstorm flattened the parsley beds at the herb garden.

A cheesemaker matured twenty rounds of cheese at the goat farm. The goat farm bought a milking machine from a dealer in Cork. A goat escaped through a broken fence at the goat farm. A school class watched the milking at the goat farm.

A retired teacher grafted a rare pear variety at the riverside orchard. The riverside orchard planted a row of old apple trees. A cider maker bought two tons of fruit from the riverside orchard. A late frost damaged the blossom at the riverside orchard.

A technician replaced the anemometer at the coastal weather station. The coastal weather station recorded the strongest gust of the winter. A film crew interviewed the staff of the coastal weather station. A fox dug a den under the fence of the coastal weather station.

A poet read new work at the corner bookshop. The corner bookshop ordered a crate of atlases from a publisher in Bern. A customer sold a box of paperbacks to the corner bookshop. A radiator leaked onto the travel shelf of the corner bookshop.

A pupil passed a cello exam at the town music school. The town music school tuned every piano in the practice rooms. A donor gave a set of timpani to the town music school. A quartet from Vilnius gave a concert at the town music school.

A band from Havana played son at the old dance hall. The old dance hall polished the wooden floor with fresh wax. A photographer recorded a tango evening at the old dance hall. A council inspector checked the fire exits of the old dance hall.

A trainer taped the hands of a young boxer at the boxing club. The boxing club replaced the ropes of the main ring. A local butcher sponsored the boxing club for a season. A journalist wrote a profile of the boxing club.

A worker sorted a load of hotel sheets at the town laundry. The town laundry installed a new steam press in the back room. A customer left a silk dress at the town laundry. A fault in a dryer filled the town laundry with smoke.

A cook fried a stack of pancakes at the roadside diner. The roadside diner replaced the neon sign above the door. A trucker forgot a leather wallet at the roadside diner. A blizzard closed the road beside the roadside diner.

A snowplough cleared the road over the mountain pass. The mountain pass carried a cycling race in the summer. A geologist collected rock samples beside the mountain pass. A landslide blocked the road over the mountain pass.

A student uncovered a fossil fern at the coastal dig site. The coastal dig site produced a jawbone of an ancient reptile. A television crew filmed the excavation at the coastal dig site. A high tide flooded a trench at the coastal dig site.

A technician froze a batch of wheat seeds at the alpine seed bank. The alpine seed bank received a shipment of wild barley from Anatolia. A documentary team filmed the storage vault of the alpine seed bank. A generator failed during a cold night at the alpine seed bank.

A volunteer ringed forty warblers at the coastal bird observatory. The coastal bird observatory built a new hide beside the lagoon. A rare hoopoe appeared at the coastal bird observatory. A photographer from Cardiff spent a week at the coastal bird observatory.

A luthier carved a maple bridge at the violin workshop. The violin workshop repaired a cracked cello for an orchestra in Brno. A student bought a horsehair bow at the violin workshop. A dry winter cracked the varnish on a display instrument at the violin workshop.

A miner drilled a new gallery at the deep coal mine. The deep coal mine installed a ventilation fan near the main shaft. A historian interviewed retired workers of the deep coal mine. A flood filled the lowest tunnel of the deep coal mine.

A picker filled a basket with young leaves at the mountain tea plantation. The mountain tea plantation built a drying shed near the main track. A buyer from Hamburg tasted the new harvest at the mountain tea plantation. An elephant walked through the lower terraces of the mountain tea plantation.

A pupil won a Latin prize at the old boarding school. The old boarding school converted a stable into an art room. A gale brought down a cedar in the grounds of the old boarding school. A former student donated a telescope to the old boarding school.

A carpenter built a fake staircase at the film studio. The film studio rented a warehouse beside the canal. A composer recorded an orchestral score at the film studio. A fire alarm emptied the film studio for an hour.
